node.html: We developed logic-based tools that enforce data protection policies at runtime and model-driven methods that integrate privacy models into system design. In this work, we report on the methods, tools, and practical experiences applying them.

frontmatter.title: Proving correctness for SQL implementations of OCL constraints

node.html: In the context of the model-driven development of data-centric applications, OCL constraints play a major role in adding precision to the source models (e.g., data models and security models). Several code-generators have been proposed to bridge the gap We propose a model-based methodology to prove the correctness of manually written SQL implementations of OCL constraints in data-centric applications. The approach maps a subset of SQL and OCL into many-sorted first-order logic, enabling SMT solvers to automatically verify correctness. Several examples and supporting tools demonstrate the applicability of the method.

We propose a model-driven development methodology that integrates a formal privacy model specifies policies, and semantic-preserving transformations generate implementations that enforce them by design. We implement model transformations targeting C# and Python and evaluate the approach on three case studies.

frontmatter.title: Is modelling access control worth it?

node.html: In this paper, we compare the result of implementing the same functional and security requirements by multiple developer groups in the context of a security engineering graduate course. We thereby obtain evidence on the security and efficiency of a tool-based model-driven approach to security from the literature compared to a direct implementation in a well-known, modern web-development framework.

frontmatter.title: An extended model-based characterization of fine-grained access control for SQL queries

node.html: In the context of a project in model-driven security that focuses on the development of model-driven techniques for building secure data-centric (web) applications, we extend, in three (inter-related) dimensions, a recently proposed model-based characterization of fine-grained access control (FGAC) authorization for SQL queries.

frontmatter.title: Optimising Fine-Grained Access Control Policy Enforcement for Database Queries. A Model-Driven Approach

node.html: We propose a model-driven approach for enforcing fine-grained access control (FGAC) in SQL queries using a function that generates secure stored procedures. To improve performance, we introduce a model-based optimization methodology that removes unnecessary authorization checks. Using a mapping from OCL to many-sorted first-order logic, SMT solvers can prove when such checks are redundant. A case study demonstrates the applicability of the approach.

frontmatter.title: Intelligently enforcement of Fine-Grained Access Control for SQL queries

node.html: My proposal for a model-driven methodology for minimizing the performance impact of enforcing FGAC policies using SQLSI on databases.

frontmatter.title: A Model-Driven Approach for Enforcing Fine-Grained Access Control for SQL Queries (extended version)

node.html: A model-based methodology for developing an FGAC-enforcement mechanism following our previous formal characterization, extended with empirical results about the impact in execution-time efficiency.
A case to study the readiness of the state-of-the-art model transformation tools on implementing the transformation from OCL expressions to SQL statements.

frontmatter.title: A Model-Driven Approach for Enforcing Fine-Grained Access Control for SQL Queries

node.html: A model-based methodology for developing an FGAC-enforcement mechanism following our previous formal characterization.

frontmatter.title: Model-based Characterization of Fine-Grained Access Control Authorization for SQL Queries

node.html: A formal characterization of enforcement of Fine-Grained Access Control (FGAC) policies for a subset of Structure Query Language (SQL) statements.

frontmatter.title: OCL2PSQL: An OCL-to-SQL Code-Generator for Model-Driven Engineering
frontmatter.company: International Conference on Future Data and Security Engineering

node.html: A novel formal mappings from the Object Constraint Language (OCL) to the Structure Query Language (SQL) that overcomes some of the limitations of the previous proposed mappings.

frontmatter.title: Mapping OCL into SQL: Challenges and Opportunities Ahead

node.html: A review/discussion, in the light of model-driven engineering, some of the challenges and opportunities of the mapping from the Object Constraint Language (OCL) to the Structure Query Language (SQL).
